title: Sunstone
content:
{{Top|ga|legends=Sun-stone}} {{Substance |image= |name=Sunstone |locations=[[E'ronoh]]''[[The High Republic: Cataclysm]]'' |date= |discovered= |creators= |created= |type=[[Crystal]]''[[The Acolyte: Wayseeker]]'' |color= |properties=Glowed with light |uses=*Decoration *Jewelry *Focus and strengthen energy |cost= }} {{Quote|Is that truly a sunstone?''"
"''Oh. Yes. This will do. Consider us even.''"
"''Good. I wouldn't want to feel indebted to you.|Vernestra Rwoh and Deva Lompop, after the former gave the latter sunstone|}} '''Sunstones''' were a type of valuable [[crystal]] that could be used for decoration, jewelry, and to focus and strengthen energy. In [[382 BBY]], some sunstone was brought to [[Eirie]]—the [[moon]] of E'ronoh and its [[Double planet|twin planet]] [[Eiram]]—to decorate a platform for a celebration. At some point between around [[170 BBY]] and around [[160 BBY]], [[Jedi]] [[Wayseeker]] [[Vernestra Rwoh]] was given one of the [[sunstones of Dytrew]]—albeit a flawed one—by [[Dytrew]]'s [[Keepers of Dytrew]] for saving their villages from a vicious creature. Around 160 [['ABY-BBY' dating system|BBY]], she gave the sunstone to the [[bounty hunter]] [[Deva Lompop]] as [[Currency|compensation]] for the latter's assistance. Lompop was pleased with the stone as payment, unaware she had been given a worthless crystal. ==Description== Suntones were a type of [[crystal]] that visually appeared to have a light glowing within. The crystals could be used for decoration, jewelry, and to focus and strengthen energy. However, if the crystal had a flaw in it, it could no longer be used for the latter energy-related usage. Sunstones could be found on the [[Outer Rim Territories|Outer Rim]] [[planet]] [[E'ronoh]]. ==History== {{Quote|I cannot believe you gave that woman a sunstone. I heard you as I was leaving, you know.''"
"''It's just a pretty rock. There's a flaw in the crystal that renders it valueless. I only took it from the Keepers of Dytrew because they assured me it was worthless.|Vernestra Rwoh and Ty Yorrick about the sunstone the former had given to Deva Lompop|}} By the time of the [[High Republic Era]], sunstones were considered valuable. In [[382 BBY]],''[[Star Wars: The High Republic Character Encyclopedia]]'' dates the events of ''[[The High Republic: Cataclysm]]'' to [[382 BBY]]. some sunstones were brought to [[Eirie]]—the [[moon]] of E'ronoh and its [[Double planet|twin planet]] [[Eiram]]—to decorate a platform celebrating the opening of a [[Hyperspace route|hyperlane]] connecting the two worlds to the wider [[The galaxy|galaxy]]. [[File:DevaLompop-WOTBHJabbaTheHutt1.jpg|thumb|right|300px|Vernestra Rwoh gave a flawed sunstone to Deva Lompop (pictured) as payment, though the latter did not realize the crystal was worthless.]] At some point between around [[170 BBY]] and around [[160 BBY]],{{WayseekerDate}} The novel also establishes that [[Vernestra Rwoh]] visited [[Dytrew]] at some point after becoming a [[Wayseeker]] ten [[Standard year|years]] before the book's start, thus placing her time at Dytrew to between around [[170 BBY]] and around 160 [['ABY-BBY' dating system|BBY]]. [[Jedi]] [[Wayseeker]] [[Vernestra Rwoh]] visited [[Dytrew]] and helped the [[Homeworld|natives]] repel a vicious creature attacking their villages. As thanks, the [[Keepers of Dytrew]] gave Rwoh one of the [[sunstones of Dytrew]]. Though she initially refused to accept, they assured her that the crystal had a flaw that made it worthless, which eventually convinced her to take the gift. Around 160 [['ABY-BBY' dating system|BBY]], Rwoh gave the [[bounty hunter]] [[Deva Lompop]] the flawed sunstone as [[Currency|payment]] for information about the [[scientist]] [[Florinda Jackard]], whom she was [[Investigation into the production of 47 lightsaber nullifiers|searching]] for and whom Lompop had also been [[Bounty|hired]] to find. The bounty hunter was pleased with the compensation for the payment she would have lost out on, unaware of the flaw in the crystal. After Rwoh and her companions left Lompop, she explained to the [[Jedi Knight]] [[Indara]] and the [[Force-sensitive]] operative [[Ty Yorrick]] that the crystal she had received from the Keepers of Dytrew was worthless, which was why she did not mind parting with it. Yorrick was surprised and thanked Rwoh for duping Lompop. ==Behind the scenes== In the current ''[[Star Wars]]'' [[canon]], sunstone first appeared in [[The High Republic: Cataclysm|''Cataclysm'']], a novel written by [[Lydia Kang]] and published in [[2023]] as part of the ''[[Star Wars: The High Republic]]'' multimedia project's [[Phase II: Quest of the Jedi|Phase II]].{{HighRepublicReaderGuide}} In the [[Star Wars Legends|''Star Wars'' Legends]] continuity, a [[sun-stone]] was a type of [[Gemstone/Legends|jewel]] first mentioned in ''[[Lando Calrissian and the Starcave of ThonBoka]]'', a [[1983]] novel by [[L. Neil Smith]] and the third and final entry in the series ''[[Star Wars: The Lando Calrissian Adventures]]''.''[[Lando Calrissian and the Starcave of ThonBoka]]'' Its first full appearance was in ''[[The New Rebellion]]'', a [[1996]] novel by [[Kristine Kathryn Rusch]].''[[The New Rebellion]]'' In the real world, a [[Wikipedia:Sunstone|sunstone]] is a type of gemstone used in jewelry.{{WebCite|url=https://geology.com/gemstones/sunstone|text=Sunstone – The gemstone with the aventurescent surprise!|author=[https://geology.com/authors/hobart-king Hobart M. King]|work=[https://geology.com geology.com]|archivedate=20230927120800}} ==Appearances== *''[[The High Republic: Cataclysm]]'' {{Ab|The High Republic: Cataclysm (audiobook)}} {{1st}} *''[[The Acolyte: Wayseeker]]'' {{Ab|The Acolyte: Wayseeker (audiobook)}} ==Notes and references== {{Reflist}} ==External links== *{{WP|Sunstone}} [[Category:Crystals]] [[Category:E'ronoh culture]]
